mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a monetary easing cycle reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which triggers short-term risk-on positioning and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >50% of easing cycle observations, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with persistently low federal funds rates indicate negative real interest rates and delayed policy tightening, which erodes purchasing power and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation/low-rate periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the rank correlation with 20-day returns remains below 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals severe economic deceleration and recessionary risk, which forces equity market repricing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >60% of sub-1% GDP growth quarters, or the threshold signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising unemployment rates indicate a broad-based supply-side contraction and labor market deterioration, which suppresses corporate output and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power to a baseline macro model.
